title: Minimizing acetate formation from overflow metabolism in E. coli: comparison of genetic engineering strategies to improve robustness toward sugar gradients in large-scale fermentation processes
claim_supported: Industrial study comparing three E. coli metabolic strategies to mitigate acetate overflow under sugar gradients in fed-batch. Confirms that acetate accumulation under excess glucose is a real productivity penalty and provides candidate mathematical forms (acetate formation rate proportional to glucose pulse magnitude in carbon-limited cells). Useful as a literature anchor when a campaign manifest declares carbon-source type and concentration as load-bearing design factors.
non_claim: Gecse et al. address genetic engineering strategies (pathway knockouts, TCA flux increase) not accessible without strain modification. The acetate kinetic forms are illustrative of the class; specific coefficients for a given campaign system must be measured or fitted to historical data before being used as simulator inputs.

title: Protein production by auto-induction in high density shaking cultures
claim_supported: Public protein-expression reference for lactose autoinduction and high-density shake-flask culture. Useful as a source-metadata fixture when a public demo needs to discuss induction timing, carbon-source policy, or the IPTG-to-lactose planning lever.
non_claim: Studier reports a specific autoinduction system and expression context. This fixture does not recommend a feed recipe, induction policy, or harvest time for unrelated organisms, products, or equipment.

title: Optimization of Xylanase Production from Penicillium sp. WX-Z1 by a Two-Step Statistical Strategy: Plackett-Burman and Box-Behnken Experimental Design
claim_supported: Public CC BY xylanase fermentation study with Plackett-Burman and Box-Behnken tables. Useful as a public-paper-derived fixture for source attribution, historical-run normalization, and response-surface planning.
non_claim: Cui and Zhao report one xylanase system under their experimental conditions. The normalized table is a public planning fixture here, not a recommendation for unrelated organisms, assays, or equipment.

title: Multi-information source Bayesian optimization of culture media for cellular agriculture
claim_supported: Multi-information-source (multi-fidelity) BO for 14-component culture media (C2C12 skeletal muscle cells). Reports 181% more cells versus commercial baseline in 38% fewer experiments than DoE alone. Useful as a methodological analog when a campaign exercises multi-fidelity BO over a constrained media space. Frazier (Cornell ORIE, co-author) is widely cited as the BO tutorial reference; this paper extends the same group's work to multi-source fidelity.
non_claim: Cosenza et al. optimize mammalian cell culture media, not E. coli defined media; cell growth mechanics and nutrient stoichiometry differ substantially. The 181% improvement figure is for their specific cell line and media formulation and cannot be extrapolated to E. coli periplasmic titer. Cite as a methodology benchmark, not a yield prediction.

title: Bayesian cell therapy process optimization
claim_supported: Noisy parallel BO applied to a bioprocess optimization problem with heteroscedastic observations (biological noise), reporting up to 50% fewer experiments to reach a target performance versus industry state-of-the-art DoE. Useful as a quantitative benchmark when a Phase 2 BO step expects to reduce the follow-up experiment count relative to a pure DoE expansion.
non_claim: Claes et al. optimize a cell therapy process (not E. coli media) with a different noise model. The 50% experiment reduction is for their specific problem; the actual reduction in any other system depends on the assay noise level, the number of feasible candidates, and the quality of the Phase 1 GP surrogate seed.

title: A Guide to Bayesian Optimization in Bioprocess Engineering
claim_supported: 2026 practitioner guide to BO for bioprocess engineering from the Sargent Centre at Imperial College London (same group behind ENTMOOT). Covers kernel selection, acquisition functions for noisy biological data, multi-fidelity BO, and heteroscedastic GPs. Useful as the 2026 reference point when a methods note needs to anchor a BoFire-based approach in the current BO-in-bioprocess methodology literature.
non_claim: Siska et al. describe general BO principles; specific BoFire-implementation findings (SoboStrategy stall on NChooseK via issue #450, MultiFidelityVarianceBasedStrategy ConstraintNotFulfilledError) live in this repo's docs/BOFIRE_CONSTRAINT_PATTERNS.md and docs/ENTMOOT_SWAP_DESIGN.md, not in the guide.
